summary: Iteration 17 (unified leaderboard + new prize-split + NBA bracket + box-score adapter): All 12 listed acceptance items verified. Backend 13/13 pytest pass (3 scopes, 2 prize-split math cases incl. zero cards_cut, referrals separate, NBA playoffs structure, 6 regression endpoints). Frontend Leaderboards page renders all 4 tabs (Global/Weekly/Premium/Referrals) with pool card, prize distribution breakdown, per-row potential prize. Tab switching verified; Referrals correctly hides breakdown+note. Prize math matches spec exactly: Pos1=$1312.50, Pos4=$512.50, Pos5/15=$144.88, Pos16/20=$60.66, Pos21/100=$29.41 (with $5k cards cut); Pos21=0 when cards_cut=0.
critical_code_review_comments: leaderboard.py compute_prize_split() has a dead/confusing variable: cc_top4 (line 44) is computed but immediately superseded by cc_top4_each (line 48). The first assignment is unused — remove for clarity.

compute_prize_split() uses integer-floor division (`//`) which silently drops 1-2 cents of rounding remainder. With $5,000 cards cut: 25% bucket = $1250 / 11 = 113.6363… floored to 113 cents, losing ~$1.40 across the pool. Not a bug per spec (matches stated $113.63 per the assertions), but means the pool sum-of-payouts will be slightly less than base+cards_cut. Consider documenting the rounding-down policy or adding a 'house remainder' field.

NBA playoffs endpoint key 'basketball_nba' is checked in matches.find filter ({"$in": ["nba","basketball_nba"]}) but SPORT_TABS only adds the Playoffs tab when sport_slug === 'basketball_nba'. Ingestion will need to canonicalise NBA fixtures to sport_slug='basketball_nba' (not 'basketball'+'nba' league) for the bracket UI to ever show on a live match — currently 0 NBA fixtures exist in DB. Worth a one-line ingestion mapping.

Leaderboards.jsx PrizeBreakdown function recomputes the entire prize-split formula client-side (lines 191-201). This duplicates backend logic — if backend formula ever changes, frontend silently goes out of sync. Better to read tier-summary values from a dedicated /api/leaderboard/tiers endpoint or include them in the pool object.

leaderboard.py unified_leaderboard aggregates over predictions, fantasy_squads, wc_game_entries WITHOUT any limit on the aggregation pipeline; only the final list is sliced to `limit`. With a large user base this loads every scoring user into memory. Add a $sort + $limit in the aggregation, or maintain a materialized leaderboards collection.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all spec items visually verified except live NBA playoffs UI — no NBA matches in DB, acceptable per spec)

verification_evidence.prize_split_math_with_cards_cut: GET /api/leaderboard/prize-split?base_usd_cents=250000&cards_cut_usd_cents=500000 → Pos1=131250 ($1312.50), Pos2=81250, Pos3=61250, Pos4=51250 ($512.50), Pos5=14488 ($144.88), Pos15=14488, Pos16=6066 ($60.66), Pos20=6066, Pos21=2941 ($29.41), Pos100=2941. 100 positions returned. Matches spec exactly.
verification_evidence.prize_split_zero_cards_cut: GET /api/leaderboard/prize-split?base_usd_cents=250000&cards_cut_usd_cents=0 → Pos1=100000, Pos2=50000, Pos3=30000, Pos4=20000, Pos5-20=3125 ($31.25), Pos21-100=0. Positions 21+ correctly gated until cards spend accumulates.
verification_evidence.unified_leaderboard: GET /api/leaderboard?scope=global&limit=5 returns 5 users sorted desc by total_points with rank + potential_prize_usd_cents + per-source breakdown (prediction_points / fantasy_points / wc_fantasy_points). Top user Pos1 sees $1000 prize, Pos2 $500, Pos3 $300 etc. Pool {base:250000, cards_cut:0, total:250000}.
verification_evidence.scope_filtering: weekly + premium scopes return 200 with filtered lists. Premium-only verified: all returned entries have is_premium=true.
verification_evidence.referrals_separate: GET /api/referrals/leaderboard?limit=20 → returns separate {leaderboard:[]} structure with referred_count/active_count/total_credits_usd_cents — confirmed independent from unified leaderboard.
verification_evidence.nba_playoffs: GET /api/nba/playoffs → {rounds:[4], total_series:0}. 4 canonical rounds: First Round, Conference Semifinals, Conference Finals, Finals. Empty as expected (no NBA fixtures in DB).
verification_evidence.frontend_leaderboards: Page loaded with 4 tabs (Global/Weekly/Premium/Referrals). Pool card shows $2,500 total / $2,500 base / $0 cards. PrizeBreakdown shows 7 tiers (1st $1000 .. 21–100 $0 'unlocks via cards' faded). 5 rows rendered in Global, all with potential prize. Tab switching to Weekly/Premium/Referrals works. Referrals tab correctly hides breakdown + note + per-row prize, shows referrals leaderboard (1 row: Cloudy Admin · NG · 2 referrals · 1 active · $0.20 earned).

seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded users (5 in global leaderboard, 1 in referrals).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration17.py (13 passing). Prize-split math is exact: cents-floor division — confirm any future tier rule changes against test_prize_split_with_cards_cut. NBA playoffs UI couldn't be validated on a live match because 0 fixtures have sport_slug='basketball_nba' (only sport_slug='basketball' + league_name in {BSN,CIBACOPA,LNB 2,…}). When NBA ingestion is mapped, test bracket render on a Match Detail page using a basketball_nba match ID and verify the 'playoffs' tab renders <NBABracket/> with non-empty rounds. Leaderboards page selectors: [data-testid="lb-tab-{global|weekly|premium|referrals}"], [data-testid="lb-pool-total/base/cards"], [data-testid="lb-tier-{1st|2nd|3rd|4th|5–15|16–20|21–100}"], [data-testid="lb-row-{user_id}"], [data-testid="lb-prize-{user_id}"], [data-testid="lb-breakdown"], [data-testid="lb-note"].
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found; all 12 spec items verified.
